policies.rule: Never hardcode secrets, API keys, or credentials in source code.
policies.skill: If you need a secret or API key, always read it from process.env.YOUR_KEY_NAME. Never assign a string literal to a variable named key, secret, password, token, api_key, or credential.
policies.fix_suggestion: Move the value to a .env file and read it with process.env.YOUR_KEY_NAME.

policies.rule: Every function that modifies data must have at least one unit test.
policies.skill: After writing any function that creates, updates, or deletes data, always write a corresponding test file with at least one test case covering the happy path.
policies.fix_suggestion: Add a test file alongside this function before committing.

policies.rule: Only use libraries from the approved list: lodash, axios, zod, express, pg, prisma. Dev dependencies (jest, ts-jest, ts-node, typescript, and any @types/* package) are always permitted.
policies.skill: Before installing any package, check if it is in the approved list: lodash, axios, zod, express, pg, prisma. Dev dependencies (@types/*, jest, ts-jest, typescript, ts-node, nodemon) are always permitted.
policies.fix_suggestion: Request approval for this package from your engineering lead before installing.

policies.rule: Do not use git push --force or --force-with-lease.
policies.skill: Never use git push --force or --force-with-lease. If you need to update a remote branch, use git push and resolve conflicts properly.
policies.fix_suggestion: Use git pull --rebase then git push and resolve any conflicts manually.

policies.rule: All public-facing functions must have JSDoc comments.
policies.skill: Before writing any exported function, always add a JSDoc comment block above it describing what it does, its parameters, and return value.
policies.fix_suggestion: Add a JSDoc comment block above this function.

policies.rule: Do not use console.log in production code — use the internal logger.
policies.skill: Never use console.log(), console.warn(), or console.error() directly. Always import the logger and use logger.info(), logger.warn(), or logger.error() instead.
policies.fix_suggestion: Replace console.log with logger.info (import logger from './logger').

policies.rule: All new modules must include an architecture decision record (ADR) comment at the top of the file.
policies.skill: When creating a new file, always start it with a comment block explaining why this module exists, what problem it solves, and any key decisions made in its design.
policies.fix_suggestion: Add an ADR comment block at the top of this file.

policies.rule: Only use libraries from the approved list: lodash, axios, zod, express, pg, prisma. Dev dependencies (@types/*, jest, ts-node, typescript) are always permitted.
policies.skill: Before installing any package, check if it is in the approved list: lodash, axios, zod, express, pg, prisma. Dev dependencies (@types/*, jest, ts-jest, typescript, ts-node, nodemon) are always permitted.
policies.fix_suggestion: Request approval for this package from your engineering lead before installing.
